title: Repository Purpose, Risk Profile, and Definition of Done · Failure Case: Gate Economics
summary: Design quality gates for a new repository according to its actual failure costs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates gate economics increment in a repository engineering contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Balance detection value, feedback speed, noise, and maintenance cost.
Define machine-verifiable and human-reviewed completion criteria.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Build Gate Economics evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-gate-economics-1k77eh: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gate Economics into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-gate-economics-1k77eh:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Gate Economics model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Gate Economics applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Balance detection value, feedback speed, noise, and maintenance cost.
